title: Trained-checkpoint KV trace calibration for GQA8/KV4 follow-up
hypothesis: Before spending evaluator time on a model-native GQA8/KV4 or QAT run, the native same-sharing proxy should be calibrated against real GPT-2-family prompt-stress KV trace quantization statistics so the next dataset explicitly covers low-margin and KV-outlier regimes.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Do real GPT-2 and distilgpt2 prompt-stress KV4 trace errors remain small enough, alongside the native GQA8/KV4 proxy, to justify a model-native GQA8/KV4 quality run?
include: merged GPT-2 prompt-stress decoder quality compare with present.* KV trace summaries
merged distilgpt2 prompt-stress decoder quality compare with present.* KV trace summaries
KV4 trace mean, max, scale, and outlier rollups
explicit low-margin caution from the native GQA proxy
exclude: claiming LLaMA perplexity or task accuracy

expected_benefit: turns the synthetic native GQA8/KV4 proxy into a better-scoped scheduling decision
uses existing real-checkpoint KV trace evidence before requesting a heavier model-native run
forces the next quality dataset to include low-margin and KV-outlier stress cases
risks: GPT-2-family checkpoints use MHA rather than native GQA, so this is still not final deployability evidence
trace summaries provide distribution and quantization-error statistics, not full attention-output quality under shared K/V heads
thresholds are engineering gates for scheduling the next job, not acceptance metrics

required_evaluations.objective: Calibrate native GQA8/KV4 proxy risk with real GPT-2-family KV4 prompt-stress trace statistics.

expected_result.reason: Use real-checkpoint KV trace calibration to decide whether to schedule the heavier model-native or QAT GQA8/KV4 quality run.
